summary: The app now exposes WaitToKillServiceTimeout as a standalone service-shutdown setting instead of mixing it into the older shutdown bundle. Microsoft documents the path and behavior, and a Win25H2Clean reversible probe confirmed the current VM baseline of 5000, the app write of 2500, and a clean restore back to 5000.

setting.name: Service Shutdown Timeout
setting.casual_explanation: Windows waits a set amount of time for a service to stop cleanly during shutdown. Lowering that timeout can make shutdown finish faster, but it gives services less time to flush state and clean up.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change this when shutdown feels slow because one or more services hang for too long.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: A lower timeout can speed shutdown up, but it also raises the chance of unclean service termination and skipped cleanup work.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents WaitToKillServiceTimeout on HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control as the timeout Service Control Manager uses during restart and shutdown handling. The Win25H2Clean probe showed a live baseline of 5000 on this VM, then applied the app value 2500, and restored 5000. This record covers only the service-side timeout. The older mixed bundle that also touched current-user desktop values is now left as historical research.

allowed_values.label: Observed Win25H2Clean baseline
allowed_values.meaning: The current Win25H2Clean VM waits 5 seconds before terminating an unresponsive service during shutdown.

allowed_values.label: Current app profile
allowed_values.meaning: The app shortens the service shutdown timeout to 2.5 seconds.

targets.notes: This record uses the observed current VM baseline instead of assuming an older published default.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now writes only WaitToKillServiceTimeout = 2500 for this tweak.

windows_defaults.label: Observed Win25H2Clean baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: The current validation VM and any system that already uses the same service timeout baseline

states.rationale: The current validation VM reported 5000 before the app value was applied.

recommended_profiles.label: Observed current-build baseline
intended_for: Systems that should keep their current service shutdown timing
avoid_for: People intentionally reducing service shutdown wait time

states.rationale: Keep the observed current-build baseline from the validation VM.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to keep the current service shutdown timing intact.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want faster shutdown even if services get less time to stop cleanly.
tradeoffs: Keeps the current service cleanup window.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app profile
avoid_for: Systems where clean service shutdown matters more than shutdown speed

states.rationale: Use the current app value that halves the observed Win25H2Clean service timeout.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a shorter service shutdown wait time and accept the cleanup risk.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need services to keep their full current cleanup window.
tradeoffs: Can shorten shutdown when slow services are blocking exit.
Gives services less time to stop cleanly.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Service Control Handler Function
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\WaitToKillServiceTimeout as the service shutdown timeout used during operating system restart and says services should not raise it themselves.

evidence.title: Repo shutdown timing notes
evidence.summary: The repo notes show WaitToKillServiceTimeout = 2500 for the old tweak bundle and record a current baseline of 5000 in the same section.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean reversible probe for WaitToKillServiceTimeout
evidence.summary: The VM probe captured ORIGINAL=5000, AFTER=2500, and RESTORED=5000 for HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\WaitToKillServiceTimeout.

evidence.title: Current app implementation for service shutdown timeout
evidence.summary: The provider writes WaitToKillServiceTimeout = 2500 as a standalone service shutdown timeout tweak.

decision.why: The path and behavior are documented, the app mapping is exact, and the VM probe confirmed the current baseline, app value, and restore path cleanly.

validation_proof.notes: The reversible VM probe confirmed the current baseline, the app write, and a clean restore.
